text.title: Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every architecture-decision-records application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Accepted ADRs are immutable. Updates happen via a new ADR that references and supersedes the prior one. Deleting or silently editing an Accepted ADR is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mutable history destroys the audit trail and is the leading cause of ADR programs failing within a year.

rule.statement: ADR-0001 locks the chosen format (Nygard, MADR, Y-Statement). All subsequent ADRs follow that template. Changing format requires a new ADR superseding ADR-0001.
rule.rationale: Format drift destroys grep-ability and breaks ADR tooling (adr-lint, decay detector).

rule.statement: Every ADR documents ≥2 alternatives with explicit rejection reasons. Single-option ADRs are blocked.
rule.rationale: Single-option ADRs are decision laundering; they provide no audit value because no choice was made.

rule.statement: Status field is one of Proposed | Accepted | Deprecated | Superseded. Custom values forbidden. Superseded ADRs include a `superseded_by: ADR-NNN` field.
rule.rationale: Closed status enum lets tooling (adr-lint, decay detector) reason about the portfolio.

rule.statement: A CI lint runs on every PR that touches docs/adr/, blocking merges that violate the format, miss alternatives, or break the status discipline.
rule.rationale: Without CI enforcement the format drifts in two quarters; CI keeps the gate active without reviewer fatigue.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
